name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy - Passage 1
text:
<CLIENT>-IS-POL-00X

<!-- image -->

Abstract

This Policy outlines the security requirements to be considered while acquisition, development and maintenance of information systems in <CLIENT>.

**STATEMENT OF CONFIDENTIALITY**

All information in this document is <CLIENT> PRIVILEGED and for INTERNAL USE ONLY.

The information in this document may contain <CLIENT> proprietary trade secrets and confidential information, the disclosure of which could substantially benefit competitors. As a result, this document should not be disclosed, used or duplicated – in whole or in part without the explicit authorization of <CLIENT> management.

The information is intended solely for the use of authorized recipients. If you are not the intended recipient, you are prohibited from reading, using, disseminating, distributing and copying this document.

In accessing the document, the reader warrants that the information contained herein shall only be treated for internal u

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2_passage_3
name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy - 1 INTRODUCTION
text:
1 INTRODUCTION

This Information Systems Acquisition, Development and Maintenance Policy outlines the framework for implementing appropriate security controls to ensure that day-to-day acquisition, development and maintenance of <CLIENT>’s Information systems are carried out in a controlled and secure manner.

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2

metadata.heading: 1 INTRODUCTION

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2_passage_4
name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy - 2 PURPOSE
text:
2 PURPOSE

The purpose of this Information Systems Acquisition, Development and Maintenance Policy (“Policy”) is to define a framework for the Information System Acquisition, Development, and Maintenance process. This Policy shall ensure information assets are safeguarded against internal, external, deliberate, or accidental threats throughout their lifecycle.
Further, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO 27001 and UAE National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF).
The following are the key objectives of this Policy:
        2.1 Definition of security requirements for information systems: Development of information security requirements for new information systems or enhancements to existing information systems.
        2.2 Protection of information: Protection of information involved in application service transactions against incomplete transmission, misrouting, unauthorized message alteration, unauthorized disclosure, unauthorized message duplication, or replay.

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2_passage_5
name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy - 3 SCOPE
text:
3 SCOPE

This Policy applies to all employees, contractors and third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization’s information and communications technology (ICT) systems.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure.’

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2_passage_6
name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy - 4 POLICY STATEMENT
text:
4 POLICY STATEMENT

<CLIENT> shall develop, implement, and maintain Information Systems Acquisition, Development and Maintenance Procedure to:  
        4.1 Provide strategies, tools, and techniques to protect risks towards supply chain.
        4.2 Prevent unauthorized modification or misuse of the information in applications.
        4.3 Maintain security in development and support processes. 
        4.4 Manage technical vulnerabilities.

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2_passage_8
name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy - 5.1 Information Security Requirements Analysis and Specification
text:
5.1 Information Security Requirements Analysis and Specification

<CLIENT> shall:
            5.1.1 Identify, document and review information security (IS) requirements using various methods such as deriving compliance requirements, threat modelling, incident reviews and/or use of vulnerability thresholds. 
            5.1.2 Ensure that the IS requirements and measures reflect the business value and potential adverse business impact resulting from lack of adequate security.
            5.1.3 Identify and manage IS requirements and associated processes to ensure that they are integrated with the early stages of information systems projects.
            5.1.4 Establish a formal risk-based security testing and acquisition process if products are acquired.
            5.1.5 Ensure that the contracts with the supplier addresses the new risks introduced and the associated mitigation measures, before acquiring the product/service.
            5.1.6 Evaluate and implement the available guidance for the product's security configuration and align with that organization’s final software/service stack.
            5.1.7 Review and disable additional functionalities not used in the product to ensure that it does not introduce unacceptable additional risks.
section: 5.1 Information Security Requirements Analysis and Specification

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2
metadata.policy_name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy
metadata.heading: 5.1 Information Security Requirements Analysis and Specification

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2_passage_9
name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy - 5.2 Information Security Requirements during Acquisition and Development
text:
5.2 Information Security Requirements during Acquisition and Development

<CLIENT> shall:
            5.2.1 Define a process / criterion for addressing information security requirements during acquisition and/or development of information system(s).
            5.2.2 Evaluate information security criteria prior to acquisition and/or development of an information system(s).
            5.2.3 Conduct information security risk assessment and provide approval prior to purchase acquisition and/or development of an information system(s). 
            5.2.4 Define and establish contractual requirements with the third-parties addressing information security, confidentiality, non-disclosure, business continuity and privacy requirements.
            5.2.5 Embed security by design into all architecture layers such as business, data, applications and technology by balancing the needs for information security with usability.
            5.2.6 Secure the development environment according to the level of sensitivity, value and criticality of the information processed. 
            5.2.7 Restrict modifications to vendor proprietary software packages and limit the changes to such software through a strict change control and appropriate approvals. Where possible, request the vendors to perform such modifications.
section: 5.2 Information Security Requirements during Acquisition and Development

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2_passage_10
name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy - 5.3 Privacy by Design
text:
5.3 Privacy by Design
CLIENT> shall:
            5.3.1 Define appropriate technical and organizational measures which are designed: 
                a) To implement the Data Protection Principles in an effective manner, and 
                b) To integrate into the processing of personal data, safeguards necessary for that purpose. 
            5.3.2 Identify the available technical and organizational measures, the cost of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing personal data, as well as the risks of varying likelihood and severity for rights and freedoms of individuals presented by the processing of their personal data. If it is considered that the processing presents a high risk to individuals, a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) must be carried out in accordance with organization’s Data Protection Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-012).
            5.3.3 Apply appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure that, by default, only the personal data which is necessary for each processing activity is used, in relation to:
                a) The amount of personal data collected; 
                b) The extent of processing that personal data;
                c) The period of its storage; and 
                d) Its accessibility. 
            5.3.4 By default, restrict sharing personal data on a need-to-know basis.

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2
metadata.policy_name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy
metadata.heading: 5.3 Privacy by Design

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2_passage_11
name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy - 5.4 Securing Application Services
text:
5.4 Securing Application Services

5. **Information Security Requirements Analysis and Specification**

<CLIENT> shall: 
            5.4.1 Evaluate and implement the security considerations for application services passing over public networks.
            5.4.2 Ensure that the application service arrangements between partners are supported by a documented agreement that commits both parties to the agreed terms of services, including details of authorization.
            5.4.3 Implement resilience requirements against attacks, including requirements for protecting the involved application servers or ensuring the availability of network interconnections required to deliver the service.
            5.4.4 Evaluate and implement security considerations for protecting online transactions.
            5.4.5 Ensure that the storage of the transaction details is located outside of any public access environment, e.g., on a storage platform existing on the entity's Intranet, and not retained and exposed on a storage medium directly accessible from the Internet.
            5.4.6 Ensure that security is integrated and embedded throughout the entire end-to-end certificate/signature management process where a trusted authority is used (e.g., to issue and maintain digital signatures and/or digital certificates).
            5.4.7 Ensure that adequate controls are in place to validate data being inputted into the new applications/ systems.
            5.4.8 Implement a controlled central storage and control access for program source code in program source libraries.

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2
metadata.policy_name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy
metadata.heading: 5.4 Securing Application Services

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2_passage_12
name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy - 5.5 Training and Awareness
text:
5.5 Training and Awareness

<CLIENT> shall:
            5.5.1 Ensure that appropriate training is provided to personnel to maintain the effectiveness of the security and privacy controls implemented within organizational systems.
            5.5.2 Determine the specific training type necessary for different personnel and consider providing different training types for additional security and privacy functions, controls, and mechanisms.
            5.5.3 Provide training, education, and awareness programs for organizational personnel regarding supply chain risk, available mitigation strategies, and when they should be used.

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2
metadata.policy_name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy
metadata.heading: 5.5 Training and Awareness

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2_passage_13
name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy - 5.6 Protection of Test Data
text:
5.6 Protection of Test Data

<CLIENT> shall:
            5.6.1 Avoid using operational data containing personally identifiable information or any other confidential information for testing purposes.
            5.6.2 Protect all sensitive details and content by removal or modification when personally identifiable information or otherwise confidential information is used for testing purposes.
            5.6.3 Apply the security measures and obtain necessary approvals, at a minimum, to protect operational data when used for testing purposes.

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2
metadata.policy_name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy
metadata.heading: 5.6 Protection of Test Data

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2_passage_14
name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy - 5.7 Supply Chain Protection Strategy
text:
5.7 Supply Chain Protection Strategy
<CLIENT> shall:
            5.7.1 Introduce security requirements into System Development Life Cycle (SDLC) as part of the supplier selection and acquisition strategy.
            5.7.2 Perform a supply chain risk assessment (including sub-contractors) to identify risk and appropriate protection strategies.
            5.7.3 Establish methods for reviewing and protecting development plans, evidence, and documentation commensurate with the information system's security category or classification level. 
            5.7.4 Ensure that contracts specify documentation protection requirements.

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2
metadata.policy_name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy
metadata.heading: 5.7 Supply Chain Protection Strategy

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2_passage_15
name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy - 5.8 Reliable Delivery
text:
5.8 Reliable Delivery

<CLIENT> shall:
            5.8.1 Employ tools and techniques to determine authenticity and completeness of procured hardware/ application.
            5.8.2 Deploy configuration management tools, techniques, and measures to maintain provenance.
            5.8.3 Utilize the risk-based approach to prioritize and address weaknesses or deficiencies identified during independent or organizational assessments of supply chain elements (e.g., penetration testing, audits, verification/validation activities).
            5.8.4 Ensure appropriate security for supply chain elements or system components containing programmable logic that are critically important to system functions.

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2
metadata.policy_name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy
metadata.heading: 5.8 Reliable Delivery

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2_passage_8
name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy - 6 ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
text:
6 ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

Executives & Senior Management shall:
        6.1 Make available and support the required resources to drive secure Information Systems Acquisition, Development and Maintenance.  
        6.2 Where relevant, provide direction to and participate in reviews and oversight of Information Systems Acquisition, Development and Maintenance.
Information Security shall:
        6.3 Ensure secure coding practices during acquisition, development and maintenance. 
        6.4 Restrict access to information systems based on privilege assigned to respective personnel. 
        6.5 Establish procedures for secure disposal or re-use of equipment based on the sensitivity of stored information.   
        6.6 Ensure the right security countermeasures are adopted to protect areas that contain information and information systems.   
Human Capital shall:
        6.7 Make sure all personnel accessing and/or altering information systems are aware of and accept organization’s rules and guidelines.   
Information Technology shall:
        6.8 Configure equipment and systems to ensure secure coding practices are being followed during development of information systems. 
        6.9 Maintain the list of information assets off-site with the authorized employees and external party users.  
Employees, Line Managers and Third Parties (Contractors, Vendors, etc.) shall:
        6.10 Employees and third parties (including contractors, vendors, etc.) who access organizational premises, information and information assets or use information and communications technology (ICT) systems of the organization are responsible for adhering to this Policy and all its applicable procedures.
Legal shall:
        6.11 Identify all relevant and applicable laws and regulations to <CLIENT> and communicate to the respective parties.
        6.12 Define standard legal clauses and terms for the contract/agreement to be signed with external parties, employment contracts, confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs).
        6.13 Liaise with IS Team to define and review Information Security clauses in third-party contracts/agreements, employment contracts, confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs).
        6.14 Review third-party vendor, clients and partner contracts and involve IS Team for review of clauses concerning Information Security responsibilities.

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2
metadata.policy_name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy
metadata.heading: 6 ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2_passage_9
name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy - 7 POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
text:
7 POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS

This Policy is to be part of all Employees' and Third Parties' processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.

Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record. Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure.’

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2
metadata.policy_name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy
metadata.heading: 7 POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2_passage_10
name: Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy - 8 MONITORING AND REVIEW
text:
8 MONITORING AND REVIEW

This Policy is developed and owned by the <CLIENT> Information Security (IS) Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO.

<CLIENT> IS Team will initiate a review and of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization’s operating environment.

Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <CLIENT> IS Team at [infosec@<CLIENT>.net](mailto:infosec@cpx.net) .
